label: 2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 6

extraction.one_line_summary: KUSS Sepp won from 40-rider breakaway on Alto de Javalambre, MARTINEZ Lenny became youngest GT leader since 1904, EVENEPOEL lost 4+ minutes
tactical_patterns: Jumbo-Visma planted three riders (Kuss, Van Baal, Wiebes) in 40-rider break, overwhelming numerical advantage for stage control.
ROGLIC and VINGEGAARD bridged together from chasing peloton to limit losses, UAE isolation exposed EVENEPOEL on final climb.
MARTINEZ friction with BARDET despite DSM teamwork suggests young riders prioritize GC over domestique role even mid-breakaway.

rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: positive: won stage with 26-second margin and gained bonus seconds despite celebrating early, pure climber form exceptional
rider_signals.MARTINEZ Lenny: positive: grabbed red jersey at age 20 against team orders, willing to conflict with BARDET shows GC ambition
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: negative: lost 4+ minutes and dropped from 2nd to 9th, couldn't follow ROGLIC/VINGEGAARD bridge despite defending leader jersey
rider_signals.VINE Jay: negative: crashed out while battling for breakaway position, illness already compromising his Vuelta before exit
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: positive: bridged from peloton with VINGEGAARD despite domestiques already forward, showing diesel climbing strength
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: aided ROGLIC bridge from chasing group, confirmed climbing form despite Tour de France just six weeks prior
